summary: MAS withdrew BSI Bank's merchant-bank status and imposed S$13.3m for 41 AML breaches, citing serious failures of controls, poor management oversight, and gross misconduct by staff in 1MDB-linked flows. First Singapore bank shut down for AML failures in 32 years.

MAS ordered Falcon Bank's Singapore branch to cease operations and imposed S$4.3m for 14 AML breaches, citing a persistent and severe lack of understanding of Singapore's AML controls in 1MDB-linked transactions.
MAS imposed S$1m on DBS for 10 breaches of MAS Notice 626 on AML/CFT controls in 1MDB-related transactions.
MAS imposed S$1.3m on UBS for 13 breaches of MAS Notice 626 on AML/CFT controls in 1MDB-related transactions.
MAS imposed S$5.2m on Standard Chartered Bank for 28 breaches of MAS Notice 626 on AML/CFT controls in 1MDB-related transactions.
MAS imposed S$2.4m on Coutts for 24 breaches of MAS Notice 626 on AML/CFT controls in 1MDB-related transactions.
MAS imposed S$700k on Credit Suisse for 7 breaches of MAS Notice 626 on AML/CFT controls in 1MDB-related transactions, completing its two-year review of 1MDB-linked banks.
MAS imposed S$900k on UOB for 9 breaches of MAS Notice 626 on AML/CFT controls in 1MDB-related transactions.
Former fund manager charged for engaging in acts likely to defraud investors under the Securities and Futures Act.
MAS issued a lifetime prohibition order against Kevin Michael Swampillai for his role in a scheme to misappropriate investor monies.

Goldman Sachs (Singapore) paid US$122m (about S$165m) to the Singapore Government under a 36-month conditional warning from CAD, in lieu of prosecution, for its role in the 1MDB bond offerings. Reported as the largest such payment in Singapore at the time.
Two individuals charged for offences under the Securities and Futures Act and Penal Code relating to securities market misconduct.
MAS banned two individuals for fraud and dishonest conduct in the provision of financial advisory services.
MAS banned two former insurance agents for dishonest conduct.
Singapore Police Force and MAS jointly investigating companies under the CoAssets Group (crowdfunding platform).
MAS banned a former representative of an insurance broker for dishonest conduct.
MAS banned four individuals for market misconduct.
MAS imposed a S$100k civil penalty and prohibition orders on a former trading representative for false trading (creating a misleading appearance of price) and unauthorised trading.
MAS imposed S$2.6m on DBS for AML/CFT control breaches (Jul 2015 to Feb 2020) on accounts of 11 corporate customers linked to the Wirecard matter.
MAS imposed S$600k on OCBC for AML/CFT control breaches (Jun 2015 to Jan 2016) on accounts of one corporate customer linked to the Wirecard matter.

respondents.name: Citibank N.A. Singapore / Citibank Singapore Ltd

summary: MAS imposed S$400k on Citibank for AML/CFT control breaches (Sep 2019 to Jun 2020) on accounts of two corporate customers linked to the Wirecard matter.
MAS imposed S$200k on Swiss Life (Singapore) for AML/CFT control breaches (May 2017) relating to an investment-linked life insurance policy linked to the Wirecard matter.
MAS issued an 8-year prohibition order against a former Prudential representative who misappropriated S$117,160.94 of client insurance-premium monies between Mar 2012 and Jul 2018.
MAS issued a 5-year prohibition order against a former DBS representative following his conviction for intentionally obstructing the course of justice and related FAA and SFA contraventions.
MAS imposed a S$70k civil penalty on Tay Joo Heng for insider trading in the shares of GS Holdings Limited.

statutes: Corruption, Drug Trafficking and Other Serious Crimes (Confiscation of Benefits) Act
summary: MAS issued a 9-year prohibition order against a former OCBC representative following his conviction for offences under the Penal Code and the CDSA.
MAS issued a 5-year prohibition order against a former representative of fund manager Nech Capital following his conviction for engaging in a course of business operating as fraud under the SFA.
MAS imposed S$5.8m on Credit Suisse, the largest of nine penalties totalling S$27.45m for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case uncovered in August 2023.
MAS imposed S$5.6m on UOB for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case uncovered in August 2023.
MAS imposed S$3m on UBS for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case uncovered in August 2023.

respondents.name: Citibank Singapore Ltd / Citibank N.A. Singapore

summary: MAS imposed S$2.6m on Citibank for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case uncovered in August 2023.
MAS imposed S$2.85m on capital markets services licensee UOB Kay Hian for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case.
MAS imposed S$2.4m on external asset manager Blue Ocean Invest for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case. MAS separately revoked a related capital markets licence.
MAS imposed S$2.4m on Julius Baer for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case uncovered in August 2023.
MAS imposed S$1.8m on licensed trust company Trident Trust for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case.
MAS imposed S$1m on LGT Bank for AML/CFT breaches tied to the S$3 billion money-laundering case uncovered in August 2023.
MAS imposed a S$50k civil penalty on Tan Tee Beng for insider trading in the shares of Tee International Limited and Tee Land Limited.
MAS imposed a S$137k civil penalty on Mr Ang Yew Jin Eugene for insider trading in the shares of SGX-listed Alpha Energy Holdings Limited.

MAS issued 3-year prohibition orders against two former Great Eastern Financial Advisers representatives: Chong forged a client's signature to terminate a policy and gave false information to police; his supervisor Tham was aware and encouraged the concealment.
MAS issued prohibition orders (effective 23 Oct 2025) against two individuals convicted for insurance fraud.

MAS issued a 6-year prohibition order against a former Ray Alliance / PIAS representative convicted of forgery and cheating (pleaded guilty 31 May 2023; 15 weeks' jail). Publication date approximate (March 2025).
MAS imposed S$280k on Remsea for AML/CFT breaches (Aug 2020 to Aug 2023), part of S$960k across five major payment institutions.
MAS imposed S$260k on Arcade Plaza Traders for AML/CFT breaches (Mar 2020 to Aug 2023).
MAS imposed S$170k on J-Dee Remittance Services for AML/CFT breaches (Jul 2022 to Aug 2023).
MAS imposed S$140k on Mobile Community Tech for AML/CFT breaches (Sep 2021 to Jul 2023).

MAS imposed a composition penalty on OxPay SG (a subsidiary of SGX-listed OxPay Financial) for AML/CFT breaches. Amount (S$110k) derived as the remainder of the S$960k five-institution total reported by MAS.
MAS issued a 5-year prohibition order against a former OCBC representative convicted of an offence involving fraud and dishonesty. Publication date approximate (Feb 2022).

statutes: Corruption, Drug Trafficking and Other Serious Crimes (Confiscation of Benefits) Act
summary: MAS issued 15-year prohibition orders against a former UOB and OCBC representative convicted of criminal breach of trust, cheating, forgery, and converting benefits of criminal conduct. Publication date approximate (Dec 2022).
Kenneth Goh Jia Poh convicted of false trading via 'spoofing and layering' plus unauthorised account use; sentenced to 19 months' jail and a S$355,604 fine. Publication date approximate (2022).

Four individuals jailed for false trading in the shares of Catalist-listed Koyo International and for deceiving brokerages via the unauthorised use of trading accounts. Publication date approximate (2023).
